subject: Statistics
title: The Posterior: Your Updated Belief
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Posterior: Your Updated Belief usable.

hook.text: You thought there was a 10% chance of rain. Dark clouds roll in. Now what's your estimate?

buildup.text: The posterior combines your prior belief with the new evidence. It's your rational updated estimate.

discovery.text: With strong evidence, the posterior shifts dramatically. With weak evidence, it barely moves from the prior.

twist.text: Tomorrow's posterior becomes the next day's prior. Bayesian updating is a continuous, never-ending process.

climax.text: Given enough evidence, two people with very different priors will converge to the same posterior.

punchline.text: Beliefs should change with evidence. The posterior is how.

quiz.question: What happens when two people with different priors see the same strong evidence?
options: Their posteriors converge — strong evidence overwhelms different starting beliefs
The person with the stronger prior is always right
